description: Infrastructure as Code Gate Checks — BDI-IaC-Convention v1.0

checks.name: All Resources in Terraform
checks.description: Every cloud resource (Vercel, Supabase, DNS) is defined in .tf files. Zero manual ClickOps.
checks.verification: terraform state list shows all expected resources

checks.name: Remote State + Encryption
checks.description: State stored in S3 with server-side encryption (AES-256). No local .tfstate files committed.
checks.verification: backend.tf points to S3 with encrypt=true

checks.name: Environment Separation
checks.description: Dev and Prod use separate .tfvars files and separate state keys. No cross-contamination.
checks.verification: environments/ contains dev/, prod/ with distinct backend configs

checks.name: Secret Management (0 Secrets in Code)
checks.description: API tokens, database passwords, private keys NEVER hardcoded. TF_VAR_* env vars or CI secrets only.
checks.verification: git grep finds zero 'password=' or 'token=' assignments in .tf files

checks.name: Terraform Lock File in VCS
checks.description: .terraform.lock.hcl committed to ensure reproducible provider versions.

checks.name: Modular Structure (No Monolith)
checks.description: modules/ contains reusable, testable infrastructure components. Root calls modules.
checks.verification: modules/{vercel-project, supabase-project}/ exist with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf

checks.name: Outputs Exported for Cross-Module References
checks.description: Each module exports outputs (project_id, api_url, etc.) for root module consumption.
checks.verification: module.*.output_name referenced in main.tf; outputs.tf present in each module

checks.name: Variables Validated (Type + Constraint Checks)
checks.description: Variables define type, description, validation rules (e.g., env ∈ {dev, prod}).
checks.verification: variables.tf has type, description, and validation{} blocks where needed

checks.name: DR / Rollback Runbook
checks.description: docs/DISASTER_RECOVERY.md documents: state recovery, destroy sequence, manual rollback steps.
checks.verification: docs/DISASTER_RECOVERY.md exists and outlines state restore, resource destroy order

checks.name: CI/CD Pipeline Defined
checks.description: GitHub Actions (.github/workflows/terraform.yml) enforces plan → review → apply. No direct apply.
checks.verification: .github/workflows/terraform.yml exists with tf plan, tf apply, approval gates

gates.G1: Code review + static validation (tflint, terraform fmt)
